title: Forcepoint Bandwidth Consumption by Application Category (`forcepoint:one`)
dataSources: Forcepoint cloud SSE (`forcepoint:one`), on-prem Web Security (`websense:cg:kv`), Email (`forcepoint:email:kv`), DLP (`forcepoint:dlp`)
app: Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053); Forcepoint Web Security TA (`websense:cg:kv`); Email Security KV (`forcepoint:email:kv`)
spl:
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as in_b sum(bytes_out) as out_b by category
| eval total_mb=round((in_b+out_b)/1048576,2)
| sort -total_mb
description: Understanding heavy categories guides QoS, caching, and SD-WAN policies.
value: Network and security teams analyze bandwidth consumption patterns by application category and user, identifying excessive streaming, abnormal upload ratios that suggest data exfiltration, and users consuming disproportionate network resources.
implementation: Install the Insights SIEM app, configure HEC inputs per product line, and align indexes (`proxy`, `email`, `dlp`, `sase`). Normalize usernames to enterprise SAMAccountName.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.All_Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security

knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053) or direct integration from Forcepoint ONE SSE.
• Data in `index=sase` with `sourcetype=forcepoint:one`. Key fields: `app_name`, `app_category`, `bytes_in`, `bytes_out`, `user`, `src_ip`, `event_type`.
• Forcepoint ONE SSE processing web and cloud traffic with application classification enabled.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify bandwidth data:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as total_in sum(bytes_out) as total_out dc(app_name) as apps by app_category
| eval total_gb=round((total_in+total_out)/1073741824, 2)
| sort - total_gb
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Bandwidth by application category:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as in_bytes sum(bytes_out) as out_bytes dc(user) as users dc(app_name) as apps by app_category
| eval total_bytes=in_bytes+out_bytes
| eval total_gb=round(total_bytes/1073741824, 2)
| eval upload_pct=round(out_bytes/total_bytes*100, 1)
| sort - total_gb
| head 20
```

Understanding this SPL: Ranks application categories by total bandwidth consumption. Categories with disproportionate upload percentages (>30%) warrant investigation — legitimate browsing is download-heavy, while data exfiltration is upload-heavy. 'Streaming Media' dominating bandwidth is a productivity concern; 'Cloud Storage' with high upload volume is a security concern.

Top bandwidth consumers by user:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as in_b sum(bytes_out) as out_b by user, app_category
| eval total_gb=round((in_b+out_b)/1073741824, 2)
| where total_gb > 1
| sort - total_gb
| head 20
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Forcepoint ONE portal: Reports > Bandwidth Usage. Compare category totals with Splunk.
(b) Cross-reference with network monitoring tools for total internet bandwidth — Forcepoint should account for the majority of user web traffic.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Forcepoint — Bandwidth Analytics"):
• Row 1 — Single-value: "Total bandwidth (GB, 24h)", "Top category", "Upload ratio (%)".
• Row 2 — Pie chart: bandwidth by category.
• Row 3 — Timechart: bandwidth over 7 days by top 5 categories.
• Row 4 — Table: top users by bandwidth.

Alert: Trigger when any single user exceeds 10 GB upload in 24h.

Runbook:
1. **Streaming media consuming excessive bandwidth** — Apply bandwidth throttling in Forcepoint policy for streaming categories during business hours.
2. **Cloud storage high upload** — Correlate with DLP findings (UC-17.5.2). May be legitimate backup or data exfiltration.
3. **Single user massive bandwidth** — May be downloading large files, streaming, or running automated tools. Investigate.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`bytes_in`/`bytes_out` always zero** — Byte counting may require specific log level. Check event format.
• **Category shows 'Unknown'** — Application classification database needs updating.
• **Total bandwidth seems low** — Split-tunnel VPN or direct internet access may bypass Forcepoint.
grandmaExplanation: We measure how much internet data each type of application uses, so we can spot if someone is uploading a lot of files to an outside service or if video streaming is hogging all the bandwidth.
